plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-516 RP17.P01.M04.S07-RP17.P01.M06.S04 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-516 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17.P01 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the fourteen remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first four rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-517 handoff, also remediating the CP00-515 P3 finding by renaming the four CP515 descriptor runtime-dispatch labels to the RP17 AI Governance scope names with the contract refreshed from the factories, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-516 (RP17 AI Governance, P01 workflow permission slice; 40 units; Risk B; descriptor-only). Pack posture is correctly NON-production: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer="descriptor", status="implemented_pending_review"; command-evidence production_ready=false, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false. The "ai_governance_core_p01_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified" flag is a descriptor-verification flag, not a runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so no production_ready gate is weakened. Coverage is fully supported: 40 units (M04=14, M05=22, M06=4), deliverable distribution implementation:22/ui:6/fixture:2/test:6/hermes_evidence:2/claude_review:2, micro-title Secondary Workflow Slice:14/Permission And Audit Binding:22/Synthetic Fixture Set:4, range RP17.P01.M04.S07-RP17.P01.M06.S04 — matching the review baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json (pack at line 407), registry CP516_REQUIREMENTS, and the program contract. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are pervasively attested and enforced: service.js freezeCp516Result forces all dispatch/write/persist/audit/storage flags false; validators.js enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, customer-safe errors, no raw payload, cross-tenant denial, and no state writes per row; manifest implementation_summary carries extensive no_* attestations. Source is pure frozen-object descriptor generation; the only I/O is the test/validator reading JSON for descriptor validation, not product runtime. Hermes patterns are evidence/descriptor-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Claude remains read-only and is not final approver (descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false; no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; request authority_boundary human_final_approval_required=true), with the CP178+ forbidden-evidence list embedded; the auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid JSON) and readiness is ready with read-only tools only. Source<->contract<->plan are internally consistent: validate-rp17-ai-governance-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the contract's current_pack/program/descriptor/requirements/no_write_attestation against the factory output and asserts historical_pack_bindings tail = CP00-516, all confirmed by reading the contract (current_pack pack_id CP00-516, p01_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor present, tail binding CP00-516). The CP00-515 P3 finding (CP00-515-P3-01, RP16-style runtime-dispatch labels) is genuinely remediated: freezeCp515Result now uses RP17 AI-governance labels (dispatches_ai_governance_runtime/model_policy/citation/retrieval_scope) and the contract was refreshed; stale RP16-style labels survive only in legitimately-RP16 files (governance-core-contract.json, packages/governance/*) and immutable CP515 history, not in any RP17 file. Required post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected-absent at this pre-review stage. Dirty non-pack files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html) are correctly recorded as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and faithfully read manifests without misrepresenting CP516. One P3 defense-in-depth finding only: the explicit matter_trace_required per-row guard present in the CP514 validator was not carried into the CP515/CP516 validators; non-blocking because the value stays frozen-correct via shared ROW_EXTRAS and is independently enforced by the contract deep-equal, and the more security-sensitive cross-tenant guard is present. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: CP515/CP516 descriptor validators dropped the explicit matter_trace_required per-row guard present in the CP514 validator
findings.details: The CP514 descriptor validator enforces an explicit matter-trace guard (validators.js validateAiGovernanceCoreCp514ScopeContractFoundationDescriptor, lines ~140-142: 'if (rows.matter_trace_reference && rows.matter_trace_reference.matter_trace_required !== true) errors.push(...)'). The CP515 and CP516 row-guard blocks (validateAiGovernanceCoreCp516P01WorkflowPermissionSliceDescriptor, lines ~530-556) carry forward the tenant_scope_field, state_transition_map, validation_helper, fixture_model, model_unit_test, invalid_reference_test, hermes_model_summary, and claude_model_review_prompt guards but omit the matter_trace_reference guard, even though 'Matter trace reference' is a row in M05 and M06 (RP17.P01.M06.S04 is the pack's last unit). Impact is descriptor-hygiene / defense-in-depth only and non-blocking: the matter_trace_required=true value is still produced from the shared frozen AI_GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP514_ROW_EXTRAS and is independently protected by the validator's deep-equal of the contract's p01_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor against the factory output (any drift in the frozen value would fail the deep-equal), and the more security-sensitive tenant_scope_field cross-tenant-denial guard IS present. No data, security, or boundary integrity is affected, and no gate is weakened.
findings.recommendation: For parity with the CP514 validator and copy-paste-drift defense-in-depth, optionally re-add 'if (rows.matter_trace_reference && rows.matter_trace_reference.matter_trace_required !== false) errors.push(`CP00-516 ${microId} matter trace must be required`)' (asserting ===true) to the CP515/CP516 row-guard blocks and regenerate the contract projection so the validator deep-equal stays green. Non-blocking; suitable for a follow-up pack or acceptable to document as covered by the contract deep-equal.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-516 ai governance core p01 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
